授業目的: 本講義では学生が英語でコミュニケーションを取るための基本的な日常会話の語彙・文 法を修得することと、話す技術の向上を目的としている。
授業の概要・背景: Although there are regular homework assignments (20%), a strong emphasis is put on in-class participation and communication which is reflected in the Grading percentages (80% over a variety of activities). Students who are considering taking this course should also note that there are no Mid-term or Final Assessment examinations.
授業方法: Lecture: Faculty members explain the details of knowledge. Basically, the main learning activity for students is to take notes of the explanations made by faculty members and from videos of TED.com presentation talks. However, these are followed by question-and-answer sessions between faculty members and students.Pair Work: Students perform learning activities in pairs such as collaboration on textbook activities and the ‘Topic Talker’ speaking fluency exercise.Group Work: Students make groups of three members or more to perform learning activities such as discussion, surveying, and interviewing.Presentation: Students summarize the results of their own learning activities and plan a short presentation script to present to a partner. Additionally, students present a handmade poster to a variety of rotating partners in the Poster Presentations - Sessions 7 & 14.Assignment for next Session preparation - Textbook Homework: Students prepare for the next class by reviewing the textbook’s covered content, previewing upcoming contents, and completing assigned online Spark Homework: After registering with the National Geographic Spark website, students complete assigned tasks. Task types consist of Vocabulary Matching, Comprehension, Listening Skills, Note-taking Skills, Recorded Speaking, and Pronunciation Skills
